segments.text: The word that Yahweh spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, by Jeremiah the prophet.

segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
For a nation comes up out of the north against her, which will make her land desolate, 
and no one will dwell in it. They have fled. They are gone, 
both man and animal.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “In those days, and in that time,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: says Yahweh,

the children of Israel will come, 
they and the children of Judah together; they will go on their way weeping, and will seek Yahweh their God.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
They will inquire concerning Zion with their faces turned toward it, saying, ‘Come, and join yourselves to Yahweh in an everlasting covenant
that will not be forgotten.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds have caused them to go astray. They have turned them away on the mountains. They have gone from mountain to hill. 
They have forgotten their resting place.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
All who found them have devoured them. Their adversaries said, ‘We are not guilty, because they have sinned against Yahweh, the habitation of righteousness, 
even Yahweh, the hope of their fathers.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
“Flee out of the middle of Babylon! Go out of the land of the Chaldeans, 
and be as the male goats before the flocks.
segments.speaker: Yahweh
Yahweh

segments.text: Chaldea will be a prey. All who prey on her will be satisfied,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.

Because you are glad, because you rejoice, O you who plunder my heritage, because you are wanton as a heifer that treads out the grain, 
and neigh as strong horses,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: your mother will be utterly disappointed. She who bore you will be confounded. Behold, she will be the least of the nations, a wilderness, a dry land, and a desert.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Because of Yahweh’s wrath she won’t be inhabited, but she will be wholly desolate. Everyone who goes by Babylon will be astonished, and hiss at all her plagues.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Set yourselves in array against Babylon all around, all you who bend the bow; 
shoot at her. Spare no arrows, for she has sinned against Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Shout against her all around. She has submitted herself. 
Her bulwarks have fallen. Her walls have been thrown down, for it is the vengeance of Yahweh. Take vengeance on her. As she has done, do to her.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Cut off the sower from Babylon, and him who handles the sickle in the time of harvest. For fear of the oppressing sword, they will each return to their own people, 
and they will each flee to their own land.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “Israel is a hunted sheep. The lions have driven him away. First, the king of Assyria devoured him, and now at last Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has broken his bones.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Therefore Yahweh of Armies, the God of Israel, says:
Behold, I will punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I have punished the king of Assyria.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
I will bring Israel again to his pasture, and he will feed on Carmel and Bashan. 
His soul will be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: In those days, and in that time,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “Go up against the land of Merathaim, even against it, and against the inhabitants of Pekod. Kill and utterly destroy after them,
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: says Yahweh,
and do according to all that I have commanded you.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: A sound of battle is in the land, and of great destruction.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: How the hammer of the whole earth is cut apart and broken! How Babylon has become a desolation among the nations!
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
I have laid a snare for you, and you are also taken, Babylon, 
and you weren’t aware. You are found, and also caught, 
because you have fought against Yahweh.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Yahweh has opened his armory, and has brought out the weapons of his indignation; 
for the Lord, Yahweh of Armies, has a work to do in the land of the Chaldeans.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Come against her from the farthest border. Open her storehouses. 
Cast her up as heaps. Destroy her utterly. Let nothing of her be left.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Kill all her bulls. Let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them! For their day has come, the time of their visitation.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Listen to those who flee and escape out of the land of Babylon, to declare in Zion the vengeance of Yahweh our God, 
the vengeance of his temple.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “Call together the archers against Babylon, all those who bend the bow. Encamp against her all around. Let none of it escape. Pay her back according to her work. According to all that she has done, do to her; for she has been proud against Yahweh, against the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Therefore her young men will fall in her streets. All her men of war will be brought to silence in that day,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh.
Behold, I am against you, you proud one,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says the Lord, Yahweh of Armies;

for your day has come, 
the time that I will visit you.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: The proud one will stumble and fall, and no one will raise him up. I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it will devour all who are around him.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Yahweh of Armies says:

The children of Israel and the children of Judah are oppressed together. All who took them captive hold them fast. 
They refuse to let them go.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
Their Redeemer is strong. Yahweh of Armies is his name. He will thoroughly plead their cause, that he may give rest to the earth, 
and disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: “A sword is on the Chaldeans,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

and on the inhabitants of Babylon, 
on her princes, 
and on her wise men.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: A sword is on the boasters, and they will become fools. A sword is on her mighty men, and they will be dismayed.
segments.speaker: The LORD
The LORD

segments.text: A drought is on her waters, and they will be dried up; for it is a land of engraved images, and they are mad over idols.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
Therefore the wild animals of the desertwith the wolves will dwell there. The ostriches will dwell therein. It will be inhabited no more forever, 
neither will it be lived in from generation to generation.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and its neighbor cities,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: says Yahweh,

so no man will dwell there, 
neither will any son of man live therein.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: “Behold, a people comes from the north. A great nation and many kings will be stirred up from the uttermost parts of the earth.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text:
They take up bow and spear. They are cruel, and have no mercy. 
Their voice roars like the sea. They ride on horses, everyone set in array, 
as a man to the battle, 
against you, daughter of Babylon.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: The king of Babylon has heard the news of them, and his hands become feeble. Anguish has taken hold of him, pains as of a woman in labor.
segments.speaker: Yahweh
Yahweh

segments.text: Therefore hear the counsel of Yahwehthat he has taken against Babylon; and his purposesthat he has purposed against the land of the Chaldeans: Surely they will drag them away, even the little ones of the flock.
Surely he will make their habitation desolate over them.
The earth trembles at the noise of the taking of Babylon. The cry is heard among the nations.
